chapter_title.en: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer

text: 1087 - وَ رَوَى عِيصُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي إِزَارِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ وَ فِي ثَوْبِهَا وَ يَعْتَمُّ بِخِمَارِهَا قَالَ «إِذَا كَانَتْ مَأْمُونَةً فَلاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1087 - Iyas ibn al-Qasim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in a woman's izar (waistcloth), in her garment, and uses her khimar (headcover) as a turban.
Imam (as) said: "If She is trustworthy (māʾmūnah), there is no harm."
